layout_description:
Option: OPT-1
Slide description: FULL-WIDTH WORKSTREAM TABLE LAYOUT. The slide is dominated by a single large table (C1) that spans nearly the entire slide width and height, with a compact title/header area (C0) positioned above it. The layout uses a top-to-bottom orientation with minimal padding to accommodate the dense tabular content. The table is structured as a multi-column grid with 4 time-period columns (2026 Q2, Q3, Q4, 2027) plus 3 metadata columns (Workstream, Details, Owner(s)) on the left. Five distinct workstream rows create a 5×7 grid structure. The design follows the Flat Geometry Table pattern (Section 4 Option C) appropriate for formal project planning content: no filled header bars, accent tabs beneath column headers, light grey row label cells, and thin dashed grey separator lines between rows to create visual rhythm without consuming vertical space. All content is distributed evenly across the available slide area with minimal whitespace, reflecting the high information density of this project workplan.

Blueprint:
  Orientation: top-to-bottom
  Regions: 2
  Allocation: [0.1, 0.9]

Containers:
  C0 (TITLE AND TIMELINE HEADER AREA): Compact header zone occupying approximately 10% of slide height at the top. Contains the slide title 'Fund Accounting platform initiative workplan' on the left side and a timeline header 'Deliverables against each workstream' spanning the right portion. The title uses heading font in brand primary color, medium-to-large size. The timeline header sits aligned with the table columns below it, using body font in brand text color, slightly smaller than the title. A 'Current focus' label appears in the top-right corner as a small text element. The yellow annotation box from the source slide is removed entirely (editorial comment, not content). This area establishes the slide context and aligns horizontally with the table structure below. Minimal vertical padding between this header and the table top edge — just enough to visually separate title from content without wasting space.
  C1 (FULL-WIDTH WORKSTREAM TABLE): Dominant container occupying approximately 85% of slide height and 96% of slide width. Structured as a formal project workplan table using the Flat Geometry Table pattern (Design Guidelines Section 4 Option C). Seven columns total: three metadata columns on the left (Workstream, Details, Owner(s)) and four timeline columns on the right (2026 Q2, Q3, Q4, 2027). Five workstream rows create a 5×7 grid. COLUMN HEADERS: Plain bold text in body font, brand text color, left-aligned within each column. Below each header: a small accent tab (brand primary color, very thin height, narrow width relative to column, top corners slightly rounded, left-aligned beneath the header text, sitting fully below without overlap). Immediately below and touching each accent tab: a thin solid horizontal line in the same brand color spanning that column's width with small white gutters between columns. COLUMN WIDTHS: Workstream column sized to fit longest workstream name without wrapping (approximately 20% of table width). Details column slightly wider (approximately 18%). Owner(s) column compact (approximately 12%). Timeline columns share remaining space equally (approximately 12.5% each). ROW STRUCTURE: Five workstream rows distributed evenly across remaining vertical space using equal flexGrow. Each row band spans all seven columns at identical top and bottom edges (horizontal alignment enforced). WORKSTREAM COLUMN CELLS: Light grey filled rectangles (no border, no rounded corners, full column width, occupying most of row height) with bold text in brand text color, left-aligned with small left inset, vertically centered. Text wraps to multiple lines where needed (e.g., 'iii. Implement fund accounting platform – 3rd-party funds/BDC'). DETAILS COLUMN CELLS: Plain text on white background, body font regular weight, brand text color, left-aligned with small left inset, vertically centered within row band. Text wraps as needed. OWNER(S) COLUMN CELLS: Plain text on white background, body font regular weight. Department/role labels (Finance/Ops:, Tech:, TO:) in brand text color followed by names in regular weight. Left-aligned with small left inset, vertically centered. Text wraps to multiple lines. TIMELINE COLUMN CELLS: Bullet lists using native PowerPoint bullets (small brand-color circles), body font regular weight, brand text color, left-aligned with moderate left inset to accommodate bullets. Each cell contains 2–4 deliverable items as separate bullet points. Text wraps within cell boundaries. Bullets vertically distributed within the row band. ROW SEPARATORS: Thin dashed grey horizontal lines between consecutive workstream rows, spanning full table width from left edge to right edge. Positioned at the boundary between row bands. No separator after the final row. These separators are critical for visual rhythm given the minimal padding between rows. VISUAL TREATMENT: No cell borders except the column header underlines. No alternating row fills — the light grey workstream column provides the only background color variation. All text must fit within cell boundaries at body font size (reduce font slightly if needed to prevent overflow given the dense content volume). The table fills the available vertical space completely, with row heights proportional to content volume (rows with longer deliverable lists receive slightly more flexGrow). Horizontal alignment across all seven columns is absolute — every row band's top and bottom edges align precisely across the full table width.
  SC-BOTTOM (DECORATIVE BACKGROUND LAYER): INACTIVE. The slide is fully occupied by the title area and the large workstream table. No decorative background layer is needed — the table structure itself provides sufficient visual organization and the content density leaves no empty space requiring fill. Adding a background layer would violate the accent color budget and add no grouping benefit.
  SC-TOP (OVERLAY CONNECTOR LAYER): INACTIVE. This slide contains no cross-container connectors, arrows, or overlay elements. The workstream table is self-contained with no visual relationships requiring top-layer rendering. All content sits within the table structure.
